title: Lesson Quiz: Saving and Investing
description: Assessment of the concept that Building wealth and financial security.

choices.text: Borrowing money to buy something now
Setting money aside for future use
choices.feedback: Correct. Saving means keeping money for future needs or goals.

choices.text: Using money only for luxury purchases
choices.feedback: Saving is about setting money aside, not spending it on wants.

choices.text: Paying interest on a loan every month
choices.feedback: That relates to debt repayment, not saving.

questions.prompt: Investing usually involves more risk than keeping money in a basic savings account.

choices.feedback: Correct. Investing generally has more risk, but it may also offer greater long-term growth.
This is incorrect. Basic saving is usually lower risk than investing.

questions.prompt: What does interest mean in this lesson?

choices.text: A type of budget category used only for entertainment
choices.feedback: Interest is not a budget category.

choices.text: Money earned on savings or paid on borrowed money
choices.feedback: Correct. Interest helps savings grow and also increases the cost of borrowing.

choices.text: A guarantee that an investment cannot lose value
A financial penalty for comparing two products

questions.prompt: Why might a person choose saving instead of investing for emergency money?

choices.text: Because saving usually offers easier access and lower risk
choices.feedback: Correct. Emergency money is often kept in savings because it is easier to access and usually less risky.

choices.text: Because saving always grows faster than investing
choices.feedback: Saving is usually safer, but it does not always grow faster.

choices.text: Because investing is only for businesses
Because interest cannot be earned on savings

questions.prompt: Saving and investing can both support long-term financial security.

choices.feedback: Correct. Both saving and investing can help support financial security, though they work in different ways.
This is incorrect. Saving and investing can both play important roles in financial planning.
